title: Resinous Snappy Australian Dates Tart
description: A sweet and aromatic tart featuring caramelized pitted dates, perfect for a festive Australian evening. This Ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly dessert is sure to delight.
instructions: Roll out the all-purpose flour to a thickness of 1/8 inch (3 mm).
Transfer the dough to a 9-inch (23 cm) tart pan with a removable bottom.
Prick the bottom of the tart shell with a fork to prevent it from bubbling during baking.
Line the tart shell with parchment paper and fill with pie weights or dried beans.
Bake the tart shell for 15 minutes, then remove the parchment paper and pie weights or dried beans.
Arrange the pitted dates in a single layer on the baked tart shell.
In a medium saucepan, combine the brown sugar, eggs, heavy cream, vanilla extract, sea salt, and honey.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar has dissolved and the mixture is caramelized.
Pour the caramelized mixture over the dates in the tart shell.
Bake the tart for an additional 20-25 minutes, or until the filling is bubbly and the tart is golden brown.
Remove the tart from the oven and let it cool to room temperature.
Once the tart has cooled, use a sharp knife to cut it into 8-10 slices.
Dust the tart with powdered sugar before serving, if desired.
Refrigerate the tart for at least 2 hours before serving, to allow the flavors to meld together.
Serve the tart chilled, garnished with fresh mint leaves, if desired.
